title: Automated date population
excerpt: Hello all, my team works on a lot of production schedules and we’re wondering if there’s a way to automate boards so they automatically populate the date column based on another date. As an example, if we have an exhibit…

posts.author: musmediainterpreter (Nia Robertson)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello all, my team works on a lot of production schedules and we’re wondering if there’s a way to automate boards so they automatically populate the date column based on another date. As an example, if we have an exhibition opening on 3/24/25, we would like the board to be able to automatically fill in the date column for milestones happening at 5 weeks out, 6 weeks, out, etc. from the opening date. Is this possible? or does anyone have a hack they’d suggest to help out with this? Thanks so much!

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @musmediainterpreter

Welcome to the community! You need to add a dependency column and define the dependencies between the items. See this article: https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360007402599-Dependencies-on-monday-com
